input_value.description: Message to be passed as input.
input_value.title: Input Value

value.description: Basic RAG: retrieves chunks from the `rag_basic` Qdrant collection and answers the user's question from those chunks only.
value.display_description: Basic RAG: retrieves chunks from the `rag_basic` Qdrant collection and answers the user's question from those chunks only.
value.name: _01_rag_basic

input_value.description: Message to be passed as input.
input_value.title: Input Value

input_value.description: Message to be passed as input.
input_value.title: Input Value

value.description: Agentic RAG with four tools: Qdrant retrieval (primary), Calculator, Current Date (built into the Agent), and Tavily web search (fallback when the document collection cannot answer). Preserves the verbatim-first-query retrieval rule from 02_RAG_Agentic.
value.display_description: Agentic RAG with four tools: Qdrant retrieval (primary), Calculator, Current Date (built into the Agent), and Tavily web search (fallback when the document collection cannot answer). Preserves the verbatim-first-query retrieval rule from 02_RAG_Agentic.
value.name: _03_rag_multiple_tools

input_value.description: Message to be passed as input.
input_value.title: Input Value

value.description: Agent with a Memory read/write loop and Flow Control. Combines a Python REPL excel reader (Titanic CSV) with a Qdrant Wikipedia retriever. After each turn, an If-Else node checks for a `REMEMBER:` tag in the agent's response and — if present — writes the insight to session memory via Store Message. The Insight Memory node reads those saved lines on subsequent turns and injects them into the system prompt via a Prompt Template.
value.display_description: Agent with a Memory read/write loop and Flow Control. Combines a Python REPL excel reader (Titanic CSV) with a Qdrant Wikipedia retriever. After each turn, an If-Else node checks for a `REMEMBER:` tag in the agent's response and — if present — writes the insight to session memory via Store Message. The Insight Memory node reads those saved lines on subsequent turns and injects them into the system prompt via a Prompt Template.
value.name: _04_rag_query_transformation

input_value.description: Message to be passed as input.
input_value.title: Input Value

value.description: Agent consumes two MCP servers: Flow 01 re-exposed as `local_rag_basic` and Tavily's remote web-search MCP (`tavily_search`). Introduces the Model Context Protocol as a tool interface.
value.display_description: Agent consumes two MCP servers: Flow 01 re-exposed as `local_rag_basic` and Tavily's remote web-search MCP (`tavily_search`). Introduces the Model Context Protocol as a tool interface.
value.name: _05_mcp_basic

mcp_server.info: Select the MCP Server that will be used by this component
mcp_server.name: mcp_server

tools_metadata.info: Modify tool names and descriptions to help agents understand when to use each tool.
tools_metadata.name: tools_metadata

country.description: Boost search results from a specific country. Must be a full country name (e.g., 'United States', 'Japan', 'Germany'). ISO country codes (e.g., 'us', 'jp') are not supported. Available only if topic is general. See https://docs.tavily.com/documentation/api-reference/search for the full list of supported countries.
country.title: Country

end_date.description: Will return all results before the specified end date. Required to be written in the format YYYY-MM-DD
end_date.title: End Date

exact_match.description: Only return results containing the exact phrase(s) in quotes in your query
exact_match.title: Exact Match

exclude_domains.description: List of domains to specifically exclude, if the user asks to exclude a domain set this to the domain of the site
exclude_domains.title: Exclude Domains

include_domains.description: A list of domains to specifically include in the search results, if the user asks to search on specific sites set this to the domain of the site
include_domains.title: Include Domains

include_favicon.description: Whether to include the favicon URL for each result
include_favicon.title: Include Favicon

include_image_descriptions.description: Include a list of query-related images and their descriptions in the response
include_image_descriptions.title: Include Image Descriptions

include_images.description: Include a list of query-related images in the response
include_images.title: Include Images

include_raw_content.description: Include the cleaned and parsed HTML content of each search result
include_raw_content.title: Include Raw Content

max_results.description: The maximum number of search results to return
max_results.title: Max Results

search_depth.description: The depth of the search. 'basic' for generic results, 'advanced' for more thorough search, 'fast' for optimized low latency with high relevance, 'ultra-fast' for prioritizing latency above all else
search_depth.title: Search Depth

start_date.description: Will return all results after the specified start date. Required to be written in the format YYYY-MM-DD.
start_date.title: Start Date

time_range.description: The time range back from the current date to include in the search results
time_range.title: Time Range

topic.description: The category of the search. This will determine which of our agents will be used for the search
topic.title: Topic

value.description: Search the web for current information on any topic. Use for news, facts, or data beyond your knowledge cutoff. Returns snippets and source URLs.
value.display_description: Search the web for current information on any topic. Use for news, facts, or data beyond your knowledge cutoff. Returns snippets and source URLs.
value.name: tavily_search

extract_depth.description: Use 'advanced' for LinkedIn, protected sites, or tables/embedded content
extract_depth.title: Extract Depth

query.description: Query to rerank content chunks by relevance
query.title: Query

urls.description: List of URLs to extract content from
urls.title: Urls

value.description: Extract content from URLs. Returns raw page content in markdown or text format.
value.display_description: Extract content from URLs. Returns raw page content in markdown or text format.
value.name: tavily_extract

allow_external.description: Whether to return external links in the final response
allow_external.title: Allow External

extract_depth.description: Advanced extraction retrieves more data, including tables and embedded content, with higher success but may increase latency
extract_depth.title: Extract Depth

format.description: The format of the extracted web page content. markdown returns content in markdown format. text returns plain text and may increase latency.
format.title: Format

include_favicon.description: Whether to include the favicon URL for each result
include_favicon.title: Include Favicon

instructions.description: Natural language instructions for the crawler. Instructions specify which types of pages the crawler should return.
instructions.title: Instructions

system_prompt.info: System Prompt: Initial instructions and context provided to guide the agent's behavior.
system_prompt.name: system_prompt
system_prompt.value:
You answer user questions by combining a LOCAL document retrieval MCP tool and a WEB SEARCH MCP tool.

TOOLS
- `_01_rag_basic` (MCP) — queries the internal Qdrant RAG collection (Flow 01 re-exposed as MCP). Use this FIRST for anything that could plausibly be in the internal corpus (Titanic docs, IT-Grundschutz, internal policy, uploaded PDFs).
- `tavily_search` (MCP) — live web search. Use this for current events, facts outside the corpus, or when the local RAG call comes back empty.

BEHAVIOUR
1. Try the local RAG tool first when the topic could be in the internal corpus.
2. If the user asks for news, current state, or post-2024 facts — use web search.
3. For mixed questions ("what does our corpus say about X, and what is the current Y?") — call BOTH and fuse.
4. Cite each claim with `[local]` or `[web]` so the reader knows the source.
5. If both tools return nothing, say so plainly — never invent facts.


TOOL PARAMETER NOTES
- `tavily_search.time_range` is an OPTIONAL enum accepting ONLY these literal strings: `"day"`, `"week"`, `"month"`, `"year"`. Never pass a year number (e.g. `"2024"`) or duration (e.g. `"7d"`). If the user asks about a specific year, omit `time_range` and include the year in the `query` string instead.

tools.name: tools

node.description:
### Flow 05 — Introducing MCP

This flow demonstrates how to consume **MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers** as tools.

- **local_rag_basic** — the Flow 01 `rag_basic` pipeline, re-exposed as an MCP tool.
- **tavily_search** — a remote web-search MCP server.

The Agent picks which MCP tool to call per user turn, or calls both.

### Setup (one-time)

**A. Deploy Flow 01 as MCP**
1. Ensure Flow 01 has been run once so the `rag_basic` collection exists in Qdrant.
2. Open **Projects → MCP Server tab → Edit Tools** and toggle `rag_basic` **on**.
3. Copy the shown URL (looks like `http://langflow:7860/api/v1/mcp/project/<PROJECT_ID>/streamable`).

**B. Register MCP servers**
Go to **Settings → MCP Servers → Add MCP Server**:

| Name | URL |
|---|---|
| `local_rag_basic` | `http://langflow:7860/api/v1/mcp/project/<YOUR_PROJECT_ID>/streamable` |
| `tavily_search`   | `https://mcp.tavily.com/mcp/?tavilyApiKey=<YOUR_TAVILY_KEY>` |

**C. Refresh the MCPTools nodes below** — click each, then click **Refresh Tools** so Langflow lists the real remote tools.

### Try it

- *"What does our local corpus say about Zugangskontrolle?"* — triggers `local_rag_basic` only.
- *"What is the current state of Germany's IT-SiG federal IT-security act?"* — triggers `tavily_search`.
- *"Combine our internal docs on IT-Grundschutz with the latest BSI news."* — triggers both and fuses.

description: Agent consumes two MCP servers: Flow 01 re-exposed as `local_rag_basic` and Tavily's remote web-search MCP (`tavily_search`). Introduces the Model Context Protocol as a tool interface.
name: 05_MCP_basic
